title: HPOM-012: aider_wrapper.py — supervised Aider edit setup with scope enforcement
description: Create scripts/aider_wrapper.py: a setup wrapper for Aider-assisted edits. Checks git baseline is clean before proceeding, validates target files are within the task output_paths scope, creates a helper note in inbox/helpers/ recording the edit intent, then launches Aider interactively (not headless, not --yes-always). Does NOT automate Aider decisions — it sets context and transfers control to the operator.
acceptance_criteria: script fails if git working tree has uncommitted changes to the target files
script fails if any target file is outside the task output_paths in TASK-NNN.json
helper note created in inbox/helpers/ before Aider launches
Aider is launched interactively (no --yes-always or headless flags)
objective: Wrap Aider invocations with scope validation and helper note creation. Ensure operator retains control by launching Aider interactively, never headlessly.
forbidden_changes: Do not run Aider with --yes-always; do not auto-apply changes; do not modify local_runner.py
risk: LOW — new script; Aider still requires operator interaction
